channel.title: Photo Feeds
channel.description: Combined feed of recent photo essays from The Atlantic, The Guardian, The Boston Globe, NYT, and Reuters.

item.title: [The Atlantic — Photography] Photos: 50 Years Since Viking 1 Landed on Mars
item.description:
NASA — A panorama of Chryse Planitia, with part of Viking 1 seen at bottom. Viking 1 and its sister lander, Viking 2, returned more than 4,500 photographs during their missions.

Source: The Atlantic — Photography

item.title: [The Atlantic — Photography] Photos: 50 Years Since Viking 1 Landed on Mars
item.description:
JPL / NASA — August 21, 1976. This Viking 1 image is one of first images of a sunset as seen from the surface of Mars. The image shows the Chryse Planitia region of Mars about 15 minutes before sunset on August 21, 1976.

Source: The Atlantic — Photography

item.title: [The Atlantic — Photography] Photos: 50 Years Since Viking 1 Landed on Mars
item.description:
NASA — Big Joe, and other drift-covered rocks, photographed on August 15, 1976, or Sol 25

Source: The Atlantic — Photography

item.title: [The Atlantic — Photography] Photos: 50 Years Since Viking 1 Landed on Mars
item.description:
NASA — On Viking 1’s sixth Martian day, it imaged the planned site for its robotic arm to scoop up soil samples for several biological experiments.

Source: The Atlantic — Photography

item.title: [The Atlantic — Photography] Photos: 50 Years Since Viking 1 Landed on Mars
item.description:
NASA — Another morning vista, seen by Viking 1 on August 16, 1976, or Sol 127

Source: The Atlantic — Photography

item.title: [The Atlantic — Photography] Photos: 50 Years Since Viking 1 Landed on Mars
item.description:
Reed Saxon / AP — Dr. Thomas A. Mutch, the director of the Viking Imaging Team, discusses a photo of the surface of Mars, at a press conference at the Jet Propulsion Laboratory in Pasadena, California, on July 20, 1976. An enlargement of the first photo sent from the surface of Mars by the Viking 1 spacecraft is projected behind him.

Source: The Atlantic — Photography

item.title: [The Atlantic — Photography] Photos: 50 Years Since Viking 1 Landed on Mars
item.description:
NASA — NASA labeled this image, from August 3, 1976, or Sol 14 for Viking 1, “Early Morning Mosaic.” At left sits a boulder named “Big Joe,” about 2 meters (6.6 feet) long and lying about 8 meters (26 feet) from the spacecraft.

Source: The Atlantic — Photography

item.title: [The Atlantic — Photography] Photos: 50 Years Since Viking 1 Landed on Mars
item.description:
NASA — Viking 1 obtained this color picture of the Martian surface and sky on July 24, 1976. Camera No. 1, facing southeast, captured part of the gray structured spacecraft in the foreground. Orange-red surface materials cover most of the surface, apparently forming a thin veneer over dark bedrock. A zone of large dark boulders is present in the far field. The sky has a reddish cast, which is probably due to scattering and reflection from reddish sediment suspended in the lower atmosphere.

Source: The Atlantic — Photography

item.title: [The Atlantic — Photography] Photos: 50 Years Since Viking 1 Landed on Mars
item.description:
NASA — Original caption: “This is the first photograph ever taken on the surface of the planet Mars. It was obtained by Viking 1 just minutes after the spacecraft landed successfully early today, July 20, 1976. The center of the image is about 1.4 meters (five feet) from Viking Lander camera #2. We see both rocks and finely granulated material—sand or dust. Many of the small foreground rocks are flat with angular facets. Several larger rocks exhibit irregular surfaces with pits and the large rock at top left shows intersecting linear cracks … At right is a portion of footpad #2. Small quantities of fine grained sand and dust are seen at the center of the footpad near the strut and were deposited at landing.”

Source: The Atlantic — Photography

item.title: [The Atlantic — Photography] Photos: 50 Years Since Viking 1 Landed on Mars
item.description:
NASA — The “face” on Mars. NASA’s Viking 1 Orbiter spacecraft photographed this region in the northern latitudes of Mars on July 25, 1976, while searching for a landing site for the Viking 2 Lander. The speckled appearance of the image is due to missing data, called bit errors, caused by problems in transmission of the photographic data from Mars to Earth. Bit errors comprise part of one of the “eyes” and “nostrils” on the eroded rock that resembles a human face near the center of the image. Shadows in the rock formation give the illusion of a nose and mouth. Planetary geologists attribute the origin of the formation to purely natural processes. More recent, higher-quality images of the same rock can be seen here.

Source: The Atlantic — Photography

item.title: [The Atlantic — Photography] Photos: 50 Years Since Viking 1 Landed on Mars
item.description:
IMAGO / Piemags / Reuters — A color mosaic of Olympus Mons volcano on Mars, seen from the Viking 1 Orbiter. Olympus Mons is about 600 kilometers in diameter, and the summit caldera is 24 kilometers above the surrounding plains.

Source: The Atlantic — Photography

item.title: [The Atlantic — Photography] Photos: 50 Years Since Viking 1 Landed on Mars
item.description:
JPL-Caltech / USGS / NASA — This mosaic of Mars is a compilation of images captured by the Viking Orbiter 1. The center of the scene shows the entire Valles Marineris canyon system, more than 3,000 kilometers long and up to 8 kilometers deep.

Source: The Atlantic — Photography

item.title: [The Atlantic — Photography] Photos: 50 Years Since Viking 1 Landed on Mars
item.description:
NASA — Original caption, from August 20, 1975: “Viking 1 was launched by a Titan/Centaur rocket from Complex 41 at Cape Canaveral Air Force Station at 5:22 p.m. EDT to begin a half-billion mile, 11-month journey through space to explore Mars.”

Source: The Atlantic — Photography

item.title: [The Atlantic — Photography] Photos: 50 Years Since Viking 1 Landed on Mars
item.description:
NASA — The planetary-landing spacecraft Viking, under assembly at Martin Marietta Aerospace near Denver, Colorado

Source: The Atlantic — Photography

item.title: [The Atlantic — Photography] Photos: 50 Years Since Viking 1 Landed on Mars
item.description:
NASA — An image of the Viking 1 spacecraft in the configuration it would have on its transit to Mars. The Viking Lander sits inside the shell at top, attached to the Viking 1 Orbiter below, which went on to complete nearly 1,500 orbits after separation.

Source: The Atlantic — Photography

item.title: [The Atlantic — Photography] Photos: 50 Years Since Viking 1 Landed on Mars
item.description:
Science Photo Library / Reuters — Viking spacecraft test, 1973. Scientists examine an aeroshell used to test parachutes for the NASA Viking Mars program. The shell was lifted by a balloon to a height of 39 kilometers, where atmospheric conditions are closer to those on Mars. The aeroshell detached from the balloon and fired rockets that propelled it to more than Mach 1.5. The parachute deployed and slowed the craft’s descent back to Earth.

Source: The Atlantic — Photography

item.title: [The Atlantic — Photography] Photos: 50 Years Since Viking 1 Landed on Mars
item.description:
Lowell Georgia / Corbis / Getty — Clarence E. Bunnell sets up a model of the Mars Viking spacecraft in June of 1967, during its development phase.

Source: The Atlantic — Photography

item.title: [The Atlantic — Photography] Photos: 50 Years Since Viking 1 Landed on Mars
item.description:
NASA — Viking 1’s meteorology instrument stands aloft, on an extended boom, seen against the rocky and sandy surface of Mars on Sol 730. Small trenches visible in the foreground are left over from soil samples gathered by Viking 1’s surface sampler.

Source: The Atlantic — Photography

item.description:
Pierre Crom / Getty — A record number of visitors sought relief in the Paris Catacombs during a heat wave on July 15, 2026, in Paris, France. About 20 meters beneath the streets of Paris, the renovated sections of the underground ossuary maintain a constant temperature of about 57°F (14°C), making them one of the coolest publicly accessible places in the city.

Source: The Atlantic — Photography

item.title: [The Atlantic — Photography] Photos of the Week: Tall Ships, Cool Catacombs, Bastille Day
item.description:
Omer Urer / Anadolu / Getty — A close-up view of one of two owl chicks receiving treatment from veterinarians with the General Directorate of Nature Conservation and National Parks in Düzce, Turkey, on July 14, 2026. The chicks were found after falling from their nests when they were about a week old. Veterinarians are monitoring their physical and behavioral development to prepare them for release back into their natural habitat.

Source: The Atlantic — Photography

item.title: [The Atlantic — Photography] Photos of the Week: Tall Ships, Cool Catacombs, Bastille Day
item.description:
Thomas Trutschel / Photothek / Getty — A white-tailed eagle catches a fish in the Saltfjord, in Saltbrygga, Norway, on July 14, 2026.

Source: The Atlantic — Photography

item.title: [The Atlantic — Photography] Photos of the Week: Tall Ships, Cool Catacombs, Bastille Day
item.description:
Jan Langhaug / NTB / AFP / Getty — Norway’s striker Erling Braut Haaland carries a taxidermic raccoon as he gets off the plane after Norway’s national soccer team landed at Oslo’s Gardermoen Airport on July 13, 2026. Norway was knocked out of the World Cup in its quarterfinal match, against England.

Source: The Atlantic — Photography

item.title: [The Atlantic — Photography] Photos of the Week: Tall Ships, Cool Catacombs, Bastille Day
item.description:
VCG / Getty — A double rainbow hangs in the sky above the Gyantse Dzong fortress on July 16, 2026, in China’s Tibet Autonomous Region.

item.description:

item.description:
Steve Russell / Toronto Star / Getty — A swimmer checks out the smoke from Northern Ontario forest fires that has the Toronto skyline shrouded in haze, as viewed from Humber Park West, in Toronto, on July 15, 2026.

Gary Hershorn / Getty — Wildfire smoke shrouds the sun as it rises behind the skyline of Midtown Manhattan and the Empire State Building in New York City, as well as geese in a park, on July 17, 2026, in Hoboken, New Jersey.

item.description:

item.description:
Scott Olson / Getty — In Chicago, Illinois, Emma Kristin jumps into Lake Michigan while smoke from Canadian wildfires covers the lakefront on July 16, 2026. City officials encouraged people to stay inside because the air-quality index in the city was rated “very unhealthy,” with some nearby suburban areas rated “hazardous.”

Source: The Atlantic — Photography

item.description:
John Tlumacki / The Boston Globe / Getty — A yellowish sky from Canadian wildfires forms the backdrop for a Ferris wheel that spins at Pier 5 in the Boston Seaport on July 14, 2026.

item.description:

item.description:
Carlos Osorio / Reuters — A person points to the skyline while they ride the ferry from Toronto Island, as wildfire smoke from northwestern Ontario fills the sky, combined with an intense heat wave, prompting health warnings and calls for residents to limit outdoor activity in Toronto, Ontario, Canada, on July 15, 2026.

item.description:
Finn Gomez / Getty — Smoke from massive wildfires in Canada and Minnesota engulf the Washington, D.C., skyline, reducing visibility and casting a colored haze over the U.S. Marine Corps War Memorial as the sun rises on July 17, 2026.

Ryan Garza / USA Today / Reuters — People wear masks to protect themselves from wildfire smoke while attending the Ann Arbor Art Fair in downtown Ann Arbor, Michigan, on July 16, 2026.

Stephen Maturen / Getty — The sun sets behind a layer of smoke on July 15, 2026, in Minneapolis, Minnesota.

item.title: [The Atlantic — Photography] Photos: Wildfire Smoke Blankets Parts of North America
item.description:
Cole Burston / AFP / Getty — People walk in downtown Toronto as smoke from forest fires in northern Ontario causes poor air quality over the city, in Toronto, Ontario, Canada, on July 15, 2026.

item.title: [The Guardian — Photos of the Day] The week around the world in 20 pictures
item.source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day
item.description:
Buenos Aires, Argentina — Fans climb on a bus stop in Buenos Aires after Argentina’s victory over England in the World Cup semi-final. Argentina dug deep in the closing stages of the match to complete one of their greatest fightbacks. That it came against England, the old enemy, made it even sweeter. — Photograph: Rodrigo Abd/AP

Source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day

item.title: [The Guardian — Photos of the Day] The week around the world in 20 pictures
item.source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day
item.description:
Arlington, Texas, US — France’s Désiré Doué covers his face at full-time after his side’s defeat against Spain in the World Cup semi-final at Dallas Stadium. The Spanish team collectively swamped the individual flair of France, whose front four had run amok earlier in the tournament but were reduced to faulty, spluttering components by a superbly oiled machine. — Photograph: Julio Cortez/AP

Source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day

item.title: [The Guardian — Photos of the Day] The week around the world in 20 pictures
item.source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day
item.description:
London, England — People play football on pitches with dried grass after three heatwaves so far this summer, at Hackney Marshes in north-east London. The UK’s climatic extremes are becoming increasingly normal, a report has found, with last year the hottest on record and further ‘unprecedented changes’ likely to break the record again soon. — Photograph: Justin Tallis/AFP/Getty Images

Source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day

item.title: [The Guardian — Photos of the Day] The week around the world in 20 pictures
item.source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day
item.description:
London, England — Alexander Zverev of Germany serves to Italy’s Jannik Sinner during the men’s singles final at Wimbledon. Sinner described his triumph in the final as particularly special considering his painful defeat at the French Open. He recovered from a difficult first set to secure his second Wimbledon title with an impressive four-set win against Zverev, the second seed. — Photograph: Andrew Couldridge/Reuters

Source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day

item.title: [The Guardian — Photos of the Day] The week around the world in 20 pictures
item.source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day
item.description:
New York City, US — People play basketball as wildfire smoke from Canada causes hazy conditions in New York. Tens of millions of Americans faced smoky skies, irritated eyes and bad air quality as Canadian wildfire smoke spread over huge swathes of the US, affecting about 109 million people across the midwest, mid-Atlantic and north-east. — Photograph: Angela Weiss/AFP/Getty Images

Source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day

item.title: [The Guardian — Photos of the Day] The week around the world in 20 pictures
item.source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day
item.description:
Caracas, Venezuela — A boy displaced from the state of La Guaira plays football at a makeshift shelter inside a school in the aftermath of the 24 June earthquakes in Caracas. — Photograph: Leonardo Fernández Viloria/Reuters

Source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day

item.title: [The Guardian — Photos of the Day] The week around the world in 20 pictures
item.source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day
item.description:
Greater Manchester, England — Firefighters tackle a moorland blaze near the Dovestone reservoir. A woman has been charged with aggravated arson in connection with the fire, as large parts of Greater Manchester were covered in smoke. — Photograph: Temilade Adelaja/Reuters

Source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day

item.title: [The Guardian — Photos of the Day] The week around the world in 20 pictures
item.source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day
item.description:
Johannesburg, South Africa — Malawians gather in front of their consulate in Johannesburg before their repatriation. About 35,000 have already returned home after fringe groups began demanding all illegal migrants leave by 30 June, in a campaign marked by violent protests and clashes. — Photograph: Marco Longari/AFP/Getty Images

Source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day

item.title: [The Guardian — Photos of the Day] The week around the world in 20 pictures
item.source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day
item.description:
Málaga, Spain — Penitents stand in the sea during the Virgen del Carmen procession in Málaga. — Photograph: Jorge Guerrero/AFP/Getty Images

Source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day

item.title: [The Guardian — Photos of the Day] The week around the world in 20 pictures
item.source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day
item.description:
Biddeford, Maine, US — A woman prays after leaving flowers near the scene where a man was shot and killed by US Immigration and Customs Enforcement (ICE). US officials are facing mounting calls to remove ICE from the streets after federal agents killed two men who were not the target of enforcement action in less than a week. — Photograph: Robert F Bukaty/AP

Source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day

item.title: [The Guardian — Photos of the Day] The week around the world in 20 pictures
item.source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day
item.description:
Potočari, Bosnia and Herzegovina — A woman stands next to the monument bearing the names of the victims of the Srebrenica genocide in Potočari. — Photograph: Armin Durgut/AP

Source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day

item.title: [The Guardian — Photos of the Day] The week around the world in 20 pictures
item.source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day
item.description:
Bunia, the Democratic Republic of the Congo — A doctor provides care to a patient with Ebola at the Rwampara Ebola treatment centre in Bunia. There is no approved drug to help the medical teams scrabbling to save lives in the Ebola outbreak in the DRC – but there are hopes that could change within months as the first patients are enrolled in a treatment trial. — Photograph: Benediction Murhabazi/AFP/Getty Images

Source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day

item.title: [The Guardian — Photos of the Day] The week around the world in 20 pictures
item.source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day
item.description:
Noisy-sur-École, France — A sign reads ‘No fires’ among burnt vegetation and trees after a wildfire in the Fontainebleau forest during a heatwave affecting a large part of France. The mayor of Fontainebleau, Julien Gondard, said he was shocked and angered. ‘This exceptional area is consumed by flames, we’ve never seen anything like this,’ he told the local TV station ICI Paris Île-de-France. ‘The forest is fragile and it’s in a critical condition.’. — Photograph: Stéphane Mahé/Reuters

Source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day

item.title: [The Guardian — Photos of the Day] The week around the world in 20 pictures
item.source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day
item.description:
Le Vaudoué, France — A firefighter sprays water to extinguish flames in Fontainebleau forest during a wildfire in Le Vaudoué. French firefighters were tackling a blaze of unprecedented scale sweeping through the forest south-east of Paris, while in southern Spain the prime minister visited the scene of a deadly wildfire and warned: ‘The climate emergency kills.’. — Photograph: Stéphane Mahé/Reuters

Source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day

item.title: [The Guardian — Photos of the Day] The week around the world in 20 pictures
item.source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day
item.description:
Zaporizhzhia, Ukraine — Rescuers work at the site of a Russian strike on a residential building in Zaporizhzhia. Volodymyr Zelenskyy this week dismissed Ukraine’s popular defence minister, Mykhailo Fedorov, despite pleas from foreign partners and civil society for him to keep his job, as part of a wide-ranging government reshuffle. — Photograph: Daryna Naumova/EPA

Source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day

item.title: [The Guardian — Photos of the Day] The week around the world in 20 pictures
item.source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day
item.description:
Caraballeda, Venezuela — Volunteers and relatives of victims search for their loved ones amid the debris of a collapsed building in Caraballeda. There has been public anger at what many perceive as the Venezuelan government’s botched response to twin earthquakes that have killed nearly 5,000 people, with one grieving mother caught on camera berating the son of the former president Nicolás Maduro. — Photograph: Federico Parra/AFP/Getty Images

Source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day

item.title: [The Guardian — Photos of the Day] The week around the world in 20 pictures
item.source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day
item.description:
Sinjil, West Bank — A Palestinian uses a flashlight after volunteers taking part in a night watch spotted Israeli settlers on a nearby hill. A deadly campaign of extremist violence to expand Israeli control in the occupied West Bank is expected to continue until election day, set for 27 October, as settler militants and their political backers exploit their seat at the cabinet table. — Photograph: Atef Safadi/EPA

Source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day

item.title: [The Guardian — Photos of the Day] The week around the world in 20 pictures
item.source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day
item.description:
Gaza City — Smoke rises after an Israeli airstrike in southern Gaza City. The charity Save the Children has angered the UK government with a social media post marking Keir Starmer’s impending exit from Downing Street. The organisation suggested the outgoing prime minister was complicit in the deaths of thousands of civilians in the Israel-Gaza war. — Photograph: Mohammed Saber/EPA

Source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day

item.title: [The Guardian — Photos of the Day] The week around the world in 20 pictures
item.source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day
item.description:
Jerusalem — A Jewish worshipper lies on the ground during a visit to al-Aqsa compound, known to Jews as the Temple Mount, in Jerusalem’s Old City. Israel will hold national elections on 27 October, giving its citizens their first chance to pass judgment on the prime minister, Benjamin Netanyahu, and his coalition since the Hamas-led attacks of 7 October 2023. — Photograph: Naama Stern/Reuters

Source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day

item.title: [The Guardian — Photos of the Day] The week around the world in 20 pictures
item.source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day
item.description:
Gaza City — Palestinians attempt to take cover from a massive explosion after an Israeli airstrike targeting a blacksmith’s workshop in al-Sabra, Gaza City. The Gaza recovery plan being pursued by Donald Trump’s Board of Peace has shrunk dramatically from an ambitious blueprint for the reconstruction of the whole territory to a small pilot project in the south of the strip. — Photograph: Belal Osama/APA/Shutterstock

Source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day

item.title: [The Guardian — Photos of the Day] Downing Street changeover and Bolivian skaters: photos of the day – Friday
item.source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day
item.description:
Beijing, China — The former site of the Xidan restaurant, with a history of more than 300 years, reopens as a digital art and museum space. — Photograph: Song Jiaru/Getty Images

Source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day

item.title: [The Guardian — Photos of the Day] Downing Street changeover and Bolivian skaters: photos of the day – Friday
item.source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day
item.description:
Achterhoek, Netherlands — The Dutch band Within Temptation play on the first day of the Zwarte Cross (Black Cross) festival. The four-day music, theatre and motocross event is taking place in Achterhoek, in the eastern Netherlands, for the 28th time. — Photograph: ANP/Shutterstock

Source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day

item.title: [The Guardian — Photos of the Day] Downing Street changeover and Bolivian skaters: photos of the day – Friday
item.source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day
item.description:
Tiszafüred, Hungary — Birds swarm over flooded marshland at Hortobágy national park. — Photograph: Márton Mónus/Reuters

Source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day

item.title: [The Guardian — Photos of the Day] Downing Street changeover and Bolivian skaters: photos of the day – Friday
item.source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day
item.description:
La Paz, Bolivia — Women wearing traditional cholita attire prepare to officiate a skateboarding competition. — Photograph: Claudia Morales/Reuters

Source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day

item.title: [The Guardian — Photos of the Day] Downing Street changeover and Bolivian skaters: photos of the day – Friday
item.source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day
item.description:
Rotherfield Greys, UK — Parched land at Greys Court country house and gardens in the Chiltern Hills, south Oxfordshire, after weeks of hot weather. — Photograph: Geoffrey Swaine/Shutterstock

Source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day

item.title: [The Guardian — Photos of the Day] Downing Street changeover and Bolivian skaters: photos of the day – Friday
item.source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day
item.description:
Harrogate, UK — Cattle judging takes place on the final day of the 167th Great Yorkshire Show, which celebrates British agriculture with 8,500 animals across livestock classes. — Photograph: Ian Forsyth/Getty Images

Source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day

item.title: [The Guardian — Photos of the Day] Downing Street changeover and Bolivian skaters: photos of the day – Friday
item.source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day
item.description:
Royston, UK — A field of potatoes is artificially irrigated on a farm near Royston, in Hertfordshire. The scorching heat sweeping Europe has parched soils and distressed livestock, superseding the Iran war as the greatest challenge to food supplies. — Photograph: Chris Ratcliffe/Bloomberg/Getty Images

Source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day

item.title: [The Guardian — Photos of the Day] Downing Street changeover and Bolivian skaters: photos of the day – Friday
item.source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day
item.description:
South Padre Island, US — Spectators gather to watch the planned launch of SpaceX’s Starship V3 rocket for its 13th flight test. The SpaceX CEO, Elon Musk, said some engines failed to start, triggering an automatic launch abort, and the company hoped to make another attempt within a few days. — Photograph: Michael Gonzalez/EPA

Source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day

item.title: [The Guardian — Photos of the Day] Downing Street changeover and Bolivian skaters: photos of the day – Friday
item.source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day
item.description:
New York City, US — Haze from Canadian wildfires blankets the Statue of Liberty as seen from a Staten Island ferry. Much of the east coast of the US and Canada is under an air quality health advisory as smoke from Canadian and Great Lakes wildfires gradually moves through the area, creating a thick haze in the heat. — Photograph: David Ramos/Getty Images

Source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day

item.title: [The Guardian — Photos of the Day] Downing Street changeover and Bolivian skaters: photos of the day – Friday
item.source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day
item.description:
Sinjil, West Bank — A Palestinian uses a flashlight after volunteers at a night watch spotted Israeli settlers on a nearby hill and set brush ablaze to prevent them approaching. — Photograph: Léo Corrêa/AP

Source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day

item.title: [The Guardian — Photos of the Day] Downing Street changeover and Bolivian skaters: photos of the day – Friday
item.source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day
item.description:
Tehran, Iran — An anti-US banner with the Persian inscription ‘Blood for blood’ is displayed at Palestine Square. — Photograph: Fatemeh Bahrami/Anadolu/Getty Images

Source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day

item.title: [The Guardian — Photos of the Day] Downing Street changeover and Bolivian skaters: photos of the day – Friday
item.source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day
item.description:
Washington, US — A reporter from Buro, a Russian-language YouTube channel, films in front of the White House as President Donald Trump speaks in the East Room. — Photograph: Julia Demaree Nikhinson/AP

Source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day

item.title: [The Guardian — Photos of the Day] Downing Street changeover and Bolivian skaters: photos of the day – Friday
item.source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day
item.description:
Gulf of Oman — US marines from the 11th Expeditionary Unit conduct a verification boarding on the M/T Wen Yao. US forces have redirected three commercial vessels, disabled one and boarded another to ensure full compliance with its ongoing naval blockade against Iran. — Photograph: @Centcom

Source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day

item.title: [The Guardian — Photos of the Day] Downing Street changeover and Bolivian skaters: photos of the day – Friday
item.source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day
item.description:
London, UK — A removal company worker at Downing Street ahead of Andy Burnham’s confirmation as leader of the Labour party. — Photograph: Aaron Chown/PA

Source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day

item.title: [The Guardian — Photos of the Day] Downing Street changeover and Bolivian skaters: photos of the day – Friday
item.source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day
item.description:
London, UK — Media preparations in Downing Street ahead of the political handover. Andy Burnham will become the next British prime minister on 20 July, replacing Keir Starmer. — Photograph: Amer Ghazzal/Shutterstock

Source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day

item.title: [The Guardian — Photos of the Day] Wildfire pollution and clowns on a pilgrimage: photos of the day – Thursday
item.source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day
item.description:
Pontevedra, Spain — Bioluminescence known in Galicia as mar de ardora illuminates the beach of A Lanzada. The phenomenon is caused by marine microorganisms that emit light when the water is agitated, amid the accumulation of microalgae and rising sea temperatures. — Photograph: Elena Fernandez/EPA

Source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day

item.title: [The Guardian — Photos of the Day] Wildfire pollution and clowns on a pilgrimage: photos of the day – Thursday
item.source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day
item.description:
Madrid, Spain — Argentine football fans celebrate in Puerta del Sol after Argentina defeat England 2-1 in the 2026 World Cup semi-final. — Photograph: C Carvajal/JNA/Zuma Press Wire/Shutterstock

Source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day

item.title: [The Guardian — Photos of the Day] Wildfire pollution and clowns on a pilgrimage: photos of the day – Thursday
item.source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day
item.description:
Mexico City, Mexico — A group of clowns make a pilgrimage to the Basilica of Guadalupe to thank the Virgin for their health and employment. A mass was held to bless those who ‘bring happiness to people of all ages and backgrounds’ and to offer up their prayers and expressions of gratitude. — Photograph: Josue Perez/Zuma Press Wire/Shutterstock

Source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day

item.title: [The Guardian — Photos of the Day] Wildfire pollution and clowns on a pilgrimage: photos of the day – Thursday
item.source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day
item.description:
Scunthorpe, UK — British Steel’s works pictured on the day the UK government announced it was taking the company into public ownership in a bid to protect UK steelmaking. The Scunthorpe steelworks employs about 2,700 people and had been owned by the Chinese firm Jingye Group. — Photograph: Christopher Furlong/Getty Images

Source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day

item.title: [The Guardian — Photos of the Day] Wildfire pollution and clowns on a pilgrimage: photos of the day – Thursday
item.source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day
item.description:
Aydın, Turkey — A swimming pool dating back about 2,000 years unearthed during excavations at the ancient city of Tralleis. — Photograph: Ferdi Uzun/Anadolu/Getty Images

Source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day

item.title: [The Guardian — Photos of the Day] Wildfire pollution and clowns on a pilgrimage: photos of the day – Thursday
item.source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day
item.description:
Dunsden, England — The sun rises over a farmer’s field in Oxfordshire. — Photograph: Geoffrey Swaine/Shutterstock

Source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day

item.title: [The Guardian — Photos of the Day] Wildfire pollution and clowns on a pilgrimage: photos of the day – Thursday
item.source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day
item.description:
Yangzhou, China — Tourists visit the the Luyanghu wetland park, a unique ecosystem of cypress trees growing directly in the water. — Photograph: Meng Delong/VCG/Getty Images

Source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day

item.title: [The Guardian — Photos of the Day] Wildfire pollution and clowns on a pilgrimage: photos of the day – Thursday
item.source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day
item.description:
Kathmandu, Nepal — A young devotee dressed as a Hindu deity takes part in the annual Jagannath Rath Yatra procession. — Photograph: Narendra Shrestha/EPA

Source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day

item.title: [The Guardian — Photos of the Day] Wildfire pollution and clowns on a pilgrimage: photos of the day – Thursday
item.source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day
item.description:
Rome, Italy — Opposition lawmakers hold placards denouncing the Italian prime minister, Giorgia Meloni, and the government’s electoral reform bill. — Photograph: Francesco Fotia/Reuters

Source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day

item.title: [The Guardian — Photos of the Day] Wildfire pollution and clowns on a pilgrimage: photos of the day – Thursday
item.source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day
item.description:
Kyiv, Ukraine — Britain’s prime minister, Keir Starmer, and Ukraine’s president, Volodymyr Zelenskyy (right), lay wreaths at the wall of remembrance for fallen Ukrainian soldiers. — Photograph: Stefan Rousseau/AFP/Getty Images

Source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day

item.title: [The Guardian — Photos of the Day] Wildfire pollution and clowns on a pilgrimage: photos of the day – Thursday
item.source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day
item.description:
Nusairat, Gaza Strip — A woman inspects the damage caused by an Israeli airstrike on the al-Bureij refugee camp. — Photograph: Moiz Salhi/APAImages/Shutterstock

Source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day

item.title: [The Guardian — Photos of the Day] Wildfire pollution and clowns on a pilgrimage: photos of the day – Thursday
item.source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day
item.description:
Kyiv, Ukraine — Firefighters work at the site of a Russian missile strike. — Photograph: Gleb Garanich/Reuters

Source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day

item.title: [The Guardian — Photos of the Day] Wildfire pollution and clowns on a pilgrimage: photos of the day – Thursday
item.source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day
item.description:
Chattogram, Bangladesh — People gather to watch the World Cup semi-final between Argentina and England. — Photograph: Zakir Hossain/NurPhoto/Shutterstock

item.title: [Boston Globe — The Big Picture] The smoke that blanketed Boston this week has spread across the country. Here’s a look

City officials have encouraged people to stay inside as the air quality index today in the city has been rated "very unhealthy", with some nearby suburban areas rated "hazardous". Scott Olson/Getty

Source: Boston Globe — The Big Picture

item.description:
Trevor Wilkinson / MI News / NurPhoto / Getty — An Atlantic puffin returns to its burrow with sand eels in its beak, on the Farne Islands, Northumberland, England, on May 31, 2026.

Source: The Atlantic — Photography

item.title: [The Atlantic — Photography] Photos: The Wonders of Ocean Life
item.description:
Tahsin Ceylan / Anadolu / Getty — As photographed on September 10, 2025, fish swarm around part of the underwater wreck of the British cargo ship Thistlegorm, which was sunk by German aircraft in 1941. The wreck, situated about 40 kilometers west of Sharm El-Sheikh, off the coast of Egypt, is one of the Red Sea’s most popular diving spots. Armored vehicles, jeeps, and Norton motorcycles designed for the desert all lie in the dark waters, serving as a habitat for diverse marine life.

Source: The Atlantic — Photography

item.title: [The Atlantic — Photography] Photos: The Wonders of Ocean Life
item.description:
Şebnem Coşkun / Anadolu / Getty — A humpback whale surfaces and dives in Antarctic waters near an iceberg on February 4, 2026.

Source: The Atlantic — Photography

item.title: [The Atlantic — Photography] Photos: The Wonders of Ocean Life
item.description:
Tahsin Ceylan / Anadolu / Getty — A Chromodoris annae, a type of sea slug, photographed near Si Amil Island, in Tawau, Malaysia, on June 4, 2026

Source: The Atlantic — Photography

item.title: [The Atlantic — Photography] Photos: The Wonders of Ocean Life
item.description:
Alexis Rosenfeld / Getty — A reef manta ray swims in the Mayotte Marine Natural Park, in the Comoros Archipelago of the Indian Ocean, on November 26, 2017. Created in 2010, Mayotte Marine Natural Park was the first to have been created by France in its overseas territories.

Source: The Atlantic — Photography

item.title: [The Atlantic — Photography] Photos: The Wonders of Ocean Life
item.description:
Lokman Ilhan / Anadolu / Getty — A hermit crab, photographed up close, off the coast of Karaburun in İzmir, Turkey, on June 21, 2026.

item.title: [The Atlantic — Photography] Photos: The Wonders of Ocean Life
item.description:
Denis Poroy / AP — A girl watches a pod of common dolphins fleeing an orca off of Point Loma on January 29, 2024, near San Diego.

Source: The Atlantic — Photography

item.title: [The Atlantic — Photography] Photos: The Wonders of Ocean Life
item.description:
Dan Kitwood / Getty — A northern gannet flies over the sea at the Royal Society for the Protection of Birds’ Bempton Cliffs reserve on the East Yorkshire coast, on May 26, 2026, in Bridlington, England. About 20,000 Gannets, which pair for life and can live for more than 20 years, make up the quarter of a million seabirds that return to nest each summer on the 100-meter-high chalk cliffs.

Source: The Atlantic — Photography

item.title: [The Atlantic — Photography] Photos: The Wonders of Ocean Life
item.description:

item.description:

item.title: [The Atlantic — Photography] Photos: The Wonders of Ocean Life
item.description:
Özge Elif Kızıl / Anadolu / Getty — A diver swims among fish in Kicker Rock Islet near San Cristobal Island, part of the Galapagos archipelago, on February 9, 2025. Galapagos National Park spans 13 main islands and numerous islets, and is home to more than 2,900 marine species.

Source: The Atlantic — Photography

item.title: [The Atlantic — Photography] Photos: The Wonders of Ocean Life
item.description:
Alexis Rosenfeld / Getty — An underwater view of a mangrove forest in the Mayotte Marine Natural Park, seen on November 28, 2017, in the Comoros archipelago, in the Indian Ocean. The mangroves of Mayotte are home to seven species of mangrove trees. Mangroves are among the most biomass-productive ecosystems on the planet.

Source: The Atlantic — Photography

item.description:

item.description:

item.title: [The Atlantic — Photography] Photos: The Wonders of Ocean Life
item.description:
Olivier Morin / AFP / Getty — A male orca chases a bait ball of herring near Spildra Island, in northern Norway, on November 3, 2025. Dozens of killer whales and their clans follow schools of herring gathering in the fjords of northern Norway from October to January, on the hunt.

Source: The Atlantic — Photography

More than 450 others were injured when a lorry was deliberately driven into crowds of people celebrating Bastille Day on the Promenade des Anglais in 2016. — Photograph: Valéry Hache/AFP/Getty Images

item.title: [The Atlantic — Photography] Winners of the International Aerial Photographer of the Year
item.description:
Vitaly Golovatyuk / The 2nd International Aerial Photographer of the Year — Birdie in the Sky. First-place winner. Birdie is sitting on the water of a tiny, crystal-clear lake with reflection of the sky and surrounded by red grass, somewhere in Dongtai, Yancheng, Jiangsu province, China.

Source: The Atlantic — Photography

item.title: [The Atlantic — Photography] Winners of the International Aerial Photographer of the Year
item.description:
Kah-Wai Lin / The 2nd International Aerial Photographer of the Year — Thunder of Hooves. Third-place winner. At sunset in Cappadocia, Turkey, Yilki horses surge across the dusty plateau. Raised by villagers and released to roam free, they embody resilience and tradition, moving like a river of fire beneath the fading Anatolian sky.

Source: The Atlantic — Photography

item.title: [The Atlantic — Photography] Winners of the International Aerial Photographer of the Year
item.description:
Chris Gurton / The 2nd International Aerial Photographer of the Year — Potato Ridging. A Caterpillar Challenger ridges soil ready for planting potatoes while gulls circle, looking for worms in the freshly turned soil. Assington, Suffolk, U.K.

Source: The Atlantic — Photography

item.title: [The Atlantic — Photography] Winners of the International Aerial Photographer of the Year
item.description:
Koki Dote / The 2nd International Aerial Photographer of the Year — Eye. A large opening in the surface of the ice. Cracks radiating from it give the impression that something is watching from within.

Source: The Atlantic — Photography

item.title: [The Atlantic — Photography] Winners of the International Aerial Photographer of the Year
item.description:
Giacomo Feroldi / The 2nd International Aerial Photographer of the Year — Painted Fields. In spring, the fields of South Moravia in Czech Republic are filled with a variety of colors, mainly due to the sowing of rapeseed. The crops follow lines that look painted. A tree with a leafy canopy casts its shadow over the scene.

Source: The Atlantic — Photography

item.title: [The Atlantic — Photography] Winners of the International Aerial Photographer of the Year
item.description:
Kevin Nyun / The 2nd International Aerial Photographer of the Year — The Factory. Sunset light over Southern Utah’s iconic butte.

Source: The Atlantic — Photography

item.title: [The Atlantic — Photography] Winners of the International Aerial Photographer of the Year
item.description:
Koki Shinoda / The 2nd International Aerial Photographer of the Year — Commerson’s Dolphin and Giant Kelp. Commerson’s dolphins were swimming near giant kelp. The giant kelp makes the dolphins look very small. Taken in the Falkland Islands.

Source: The Atlantic — Photography

item.title: [The Atlantic — Photography] Winners of the International Aerial Photographer of the Year
item.description:
Sanghamitra Sarkar / The 2nd International Aerial Photographer of the Year — Wari. Second-place winner. A gathering of Warkaris during Maharashtra’s Wari pilgrimage forms a circular pattern around the central palkhi carrying sacred padukas. Dressed in white, devotees sit in rows, creating a powerful visual of unity, devotion, and harmony.

Source: The Atlantic — Photography

item.title: [The Atlantic — Photography] Winners of the International Aerial Photographer of the Year
item.description:
Peter O'Hara / The 2nd International Aerial Photographer of the Year — Cloud City Mount Yanapaccha. Mount Yanapaccha; 17,913 feet; Peru. Photographers stood on the summit early in the morning above a sea of clouds and sent the drone up into a magnificent sunrise. Surrounded by three dramatic peaks, it was one of those rare moments that felt almost surreal.

Source: The Atlantic — Photography

item.title: [The Atlantic — Photography] Winners of the International Aerial Photographer of the Year
item.description:
Cody Roberts / The 2nd International Aerial Photographer of the Year — Event Horizon. Like the Greek hero Icarus who soared too close to the sun, approaching the boundary beyond which nothing escapes, how close can you get? A USGS aircraft soars next to the historic eruption of Mauna Loa on Hawaii in 2022.

Source: The Atlantic — Photography

item.title: [The Atlantic — Photography] Winners of the International Aerial Photographer of the Year
item.description:
Fabio Pappalettera / The 2nd International Aerial Photographer of the Year — Tumbling White Horse. The photographer was riding in northeast Italy and catching some stills of a wonderful white horse, when suddenly he rolled over to scratch his back, raising the dust—and the epic moment was frozen forever.

Source: The Atlantic — Photography

item.title: [The Atlantic — Photography] Winners of the International Aerial Photographer of the Year
item.description:
Judith Kuhn / The 2nd International Aerial Photographer of the Year — Maloja. Fascinating from above: the serpentines of the Maloja Pass. Shortly before, it had snowed intensely for a few days and the larches had not yet completely shed their autumn dress.

Source: The Atlantic — Photography

item.title: [The Atlantic — Photography] Winners of the International Aerial Photographer of the Year
item.description:
Dylan De Haas / The 2nd International Aerial Photographer of the Year — Circle of Life. Ningaloo Reef, Western Australia. A tiger shark carries a green turtle in its jaws, revealing a rarely witnessed moment of predation. Predator and prey meet at the surface, exposing the raw tension and ecological reality that sustains ocean balance.

Source: The Atlantic — Photography

item.title: [The Atlantic — Photography] Winners of the International Aerial Photographer of the Year
item.description:
Abhijeet Sawant / The 2nd International Aerial Photographer of the Year — Embrace of the Sky. In this aerial panorama of Iceland, a gentle rainbow arcs across the sky, casting soft hues over dark slopes and deep-blue waters. A lone road winds toward distant shores in perfect, breathless harmony.

Source: The Atlantic — Photography

item.title: [The Atlantic — Photography] Winners of the International Aerial Photographer of the Year
item.description:
Yau Tat Hui / The 2nd International Aerial Photographer of the Year — Night of the Century. A tragic photo; more than 100 residents died.

Source: The Atlantic — Photography

item.title: [The Atlantic — Photography] Winners of the International Aerial Photographer of the Year
item.description:
Jiri Kostal / The 2nd International Aerial Photographer of the Year — Dead Man Walking. Self-portrait on a frozen pond

Source: The Atlantic — Photography

item.title: [The Atlantic — Photography] Winners of the International Aerial Photographer of the Year
item.description:
Laszlo Siman / The 2nd International Aerial Photographer of the Year — Vineyard. A picture taken at dawn in spring in a vineyard in the Czech Republic. The cold mist colored the surface of the earth, and in some places the ground was still frozen.

Source: The Atlantic — Photography

item.title: [The Atlantic — Photography] Winners of the International Aerial Photographer of the Year
item.description:
Zhengze Xu / The 2nd International Aerial Photographer of the Year — Oryx Walking on Dune. Taken from a helicopter in the Namib Desert, Namibia. It was late afternoon and sunset was beautiful, illuminating dunes with golden color and amazing shadow lines. This single oryx was walking on the dunes with its shadow and footprints.

Source: The Atlantic — Photography

item.title: [The Atlantic — Photography] Winners of the International Aerial Photographer of the Year
item.description:
Cédric Tamani / The 2nd International Aerial Photographer of the Year — Magadi Dream. Picture taken from a helicopter, Lake Magadi, Kenya

Source: The Atlantic — Photography

item.title: [The Atlantic — Photography] Winners of the International Aerial Photographer of the Year
item.description:
Thibault Gerbaldi / The 2nd International Aerial Photographer of the Year — Aarti Under the Stars. Each night in Varanasi, India, several thousand people gather to attend the Ganga Aarti, an ancient ceremony. Population pressures lead many devotees to follow the prayer from the Ganges itself aboard tightly packed boats.

Source: The Atlantic — Photography

item.title: [The Atlantic — Photography] Winners of the International Aerial Photographer of the Year
item.description:
Alexandra Moldovan / The 2nd International Aerial Photographer of the Year — Basking in Divine Light. Views on a moody morning over the treetops of Denmark. This light and atmosphere allowed the photographer to create a graphical, geometrically pleasing image with the sunbeams pushing through the treetops.

Source: The Atlantic — Photography

item.title: [The Atlantic — Photography] Winners of the International Aerial Photographer of the Year
item.description:
Dylan De Haas / The 2nd International Aerial Photographer of the Year — Electric Blue. Rockingham, Western Australia. Two Indo-Pacific bottlenose dolphins glide through impossibly clear blue water, their bodies suspended above shifting sand patterns. The vivid tones and flowing textures turn a quiet moment into something almost surreal.

Source: The Atlantic — Photography

item.title: [The Atlantic — Photography] Winners of the International Aerial Photographer of the Year
item.description:
Michiko Kimura / The 2nd International Aerial Photographer of the Year — Arctic Pulse Sámi Reindeer Migration. From above, a migration becomes a living heartbeat with more than 2,000 reindeer flowing northward across Finnmark, Norway. Guided by Sámi herders and ancient instinct, this seasonal movement reflects a rhythm older than borders, where survival and culture move as one.

Source: The Atlantic — Photography

item.title: [The Atlantic — Photography] Winners of the International Aerial Photographer of the Year
item.description:
Chengming Liu / The 2nd International Aerial Photographer of the Year — Emerald Waves at Dusk. Captured above the rolling hills of San Jose, California, at golden hour, this image reveals the Diablo Range at its greenest after winter rains, highlighting smooth contours and flowing patterns.

Source: The Atlantic — Photography

item.description:
Belfast, UK — Orangemen and flute band members make their final preparations before setting off for their annual Orange march. Orange orders memorialise the Protestant King William III’s victory over the Catholic King James II at the Battle of the Boyne in July 1690 with rallies across Northern Ireland. — Photograph: Charles McQuillan/Getty Images

Source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day
